text.title: TypeScript Strict Mode Configuration
text.summary: tsconfig.json compiler flags for maximum type safety, with rationale for each non-default option.

rule.statement: Always set "strict": true and additionally enable noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noImplicitReturns, and noFallthroughCasesInSwitch.
rule.rationale: "strict" covers 7 flags but leaves array access and optional/undefined semantics unchecked; the four additional flags close those gaps.

text.reference: Production-ready tsconfig with all strict flags

example.code: { "compilerOptions": { "strict": true, "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true, "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true, "noImplicitReturns": true, "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true, "noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature": true, "target": "ES2022", "module": "ESNext", "moduleResolution": "bundler", "verbatimModuleSyntax": true, "isolatedModules": true, "skipLibCheck": true } }
